    48→def is_issue_learn_plan(labels: list[str]) -> bool:
    49→    """Check if an issue is a learn plan by checking for erk-learn label.
    50→
    51→    Args:
    52→        labels: The issue's labels
    53→
    54→    Returns:
    55→        True if the issue has the erk-learn label, False otherwise
    56→    """
    57→    return "erk-learn" in labels
    58→
    59→
    60→def get_learn_plan_parent_branch(ctx: ErkContext, repo_root: Path, plan_id: str) -> str | None:
    61→    """Get the parent branch for a learn plan.
    62→
    63→    Learn plans should stack on their parent plan's branch.
    64→    Extracts learned_from_issue via plan_backend, fetches parent's branch_name.
    65→
    66→    Args:
    67→        ctx: ErkContext with plan_backend
    68→        repo_root: Repository root path
    69→        plan_id: Plan identifier
    70→
    71→    Returns:
    72→        Parent plan's branch_name if found, None otherwise
    73→    """
    74→    learned_from = ctx.plan_backend.get_metadata_field(repo_root, plan_id, "learned_from_issue")
    75→    if isinstance(learned_from, PlanNotFound) or learned_from is None:
    76→        return None
    77→
    78→    branch_name = ctx.plan_backend.get_metadata_field(repo_root, str(learned_from), "branch_name")
    79→    if isinstance(branch_name, PlanNotFound) or branch_name is None:
    80→        return None
    81→    return str(branch_name)
    82→
    83→
    84→def load_workflow_config(repo_root: Path, workflow_name: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    85→    """Load workflow config from .erk/config.toml [workflows.<name>] section.
    86→
    87→    Args:
    88→        repo_root: Repository root path
    89→        workflow_name: Workflow filename (with or without .yml/.yaml extension).
    90→            Only the basename is used for config lookup.
    91→
    92→    Returns:
    93→        Dict of string key-value pairs for workflow inputs.
    94→        Returns empty dict if config file or section doesn't exist.
    95→
    96→    Example:
    97→        For workflow_name="plan-implement.yml", reads from:
    98→        .erk/config.toml -> [workflows.plan-implement] section
    99→    """
   100→    config_path = repo_root / ".erk" / "config.toml"
   101→
   102→    if not config_path.exists():
   103→        return {}
   104→
   105→    with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
   106→        data = tomllib.load(f)
   107→
   108→    # Extract basename and strip .yml/.yaml extension
   109→    basename = Path(workflow_name).name
   110→    config_name = basename.removesuffix(".yml").removesuffix(".yaml")
   111→
   112→    # Get [workflows.<name>] section
   113→    workflows_section = data.get("workflows", {})
   114→    workflow_config = workflows_section.get(config_name, {})
   115→
   116→    # Convert all values to strings (workflow inputs are always strings)
   117→    return {k: str(v) for k, v in workflow_config.items()}
   118→
   119→
   120→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   121→class DispatchResult:
   122→    """Result of dispatching a single plan."""
   123→
   124→    plan_number: int
   125→    plan_title: str
   126→    plan_url: str
   127→    pr_number: int | None
   128→    pr_url: str | None
   129→    workflow_run_id: str
   130→    workflow_url: str
   131→
   132→
   133→def _build_workflow_run_url(plan_url: str, run_id: str) -> str:
   134→    """Construct GitHub Actions workflow run URL from plan URL and run ID.
   135→
   136→    Args:
   137→        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
   138→        run_id: Workflow run ID
   139→
   140→    Returns:
   141→        Workflow run URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/1234567890)
   142→    """
   143→    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
   144→    if owner_repo is not None:
   145→        owner, repo = owner_repo
   146→        return construct_workflow_run_url(owner, repo, run_id)
   147→    return f"https://github.com/actions/runs/{run_id}"
   148→
   149→
   150→def _build_pr_url(plan_url: str, pr_number: int) -> str:
   151→    """Construct GitHub PR URL from plan URL and PR number.
   152→
   153→    Args:
   154→        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
   155→        pr_number: PR number
   156→
   157→    Returns:
   158→        PR URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/456)
   159→    """
   160→    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
   161→    if owner_repo is not None:
   162→        owner, repo = owner_repo
   163→        return construct_pr_url(owner, repo, pr_number)
   164→    return f"https://github.com/pull/{pr_number}"
   165→
   166→
   167→@dataclass(frozen=True)
   168→class ValidatedPlannedPR:
   169→    """Draft PR that passed all validation checks."""
   170→
   171→    number: int
   172→    title: str
   173→    url: str
   174→    branch_name: str
   175→
   176→
   177→def _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(
   178→    ctx: ErkContext,
   179→    repo: RepoContext,
   180→    plan_number: int,
   181→) -> ValidatedPlannedPR:
   182→    """Validate a planned PR plan for dispatch.
   183→
   184→    Fetches the PR, validates it has the erk-plan label and is OPEN.
   185→
   186→    Args:
   187→        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
   188→        repo: Repository context
   189→        plan_number: PR number to validate
   190→
   191→    Raises:
   192→        SystemExit: If PR doesn't exist, missing label, or not OPEN.
   193→    """
   194→    pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
   195→    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
   196→        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} not found")
   197→        raise SystemExit(1)
   198→
   199→    # Validate: must have erk-plan label
   200→    if ERK_PLAN_LABEL not in pr_result.labels:
   201→        user_output(
   202→            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   203→            + f"PR #{plan_number} does not have {ERK_PLAN_LABEL} label\n\n"
   204→            "Cannot dispatch non-plan PRs for automated implementation."
   205→        )
   206→        raise SystemExit(1)
   207→
   208→    # Validate: must be OPEN
   209→    if pr_result.state != "OPEN":
   210→        user_output(
   211→            click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} is {pr_result.state}\n\n"
   212→            "Cannot dispatch closed PRs for automated implementation."
   213→        )
   214→        raise SystemExit(1)
   215→
   216→    return ValidatedPlannedPR(
   217→        number=plan_number,
   218→        title=pr_result.title,
   219→        url=pr_result.url,
   220→        branch_name=pr_result.head_ref_name,
   221→    )
   222→
   223→
   224→def _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
   225→    ctx: ErkContext,
   226→    *,
   227→    repo: RepoContext,
   228→    validated: ValidatedPlannedPR,
   229→    submitted_by: str,
   230→    original_branch: str,
   231→    base_branch: str,
   232→) -> DispatchResult:
   233→    """Dispatch a validated planned-PR plan for implementation.
   234→
   235→    For planned-PR plans, the branch and PR already exist. This function:
   236→    - Fetches and checks out the existing plan branch
   237→    - Creates .erk/impl-context/ with provider="github-draft-pr"
   238→    - Commits and pushes .erk/impl-context/ to existing branch
   239→    - Triggers the workflow with plan_backend="planned_pr"
   240→
   241→    Args:
   242→        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
   243→        repo: Repository context
   244→        validated: Validated planned PR information
   245→        submitted_by: GitHub username of submitter
   246→        original_branch: Original branch name (to restore after)
   247→        base_branch: Base branch for PR
   248→
   249→    Returns:
   250→        DispatchResult with URLs and identifiers.
   251→    """
   252→    plan_number = validated.number
   253→    branch_name = validated.branch_name
   254→
   255→    # Fetch plan content via PlanBackend
   256→    user_output("Fetching plan content...")
   257→    result = ctx.plan_store.get_plan(repo.root, str(plan_number))
   258→    if isinstance(result, PlanNotFound):
   259→        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number}: plan content not found")
   260→        raise SystemExit(1)
   261→    plan = result
   262→
   263→    # Fetch and checkout the existing plan branch
   264→    user_output(f"Checking out existing plan branch: {click.style(branch_name, fg='cyan')}")
   265→    ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)
   266→
   267→    local_branches = ctx.git.branch.list_local_branches(repo.root)
   268→    if branch_name not in local_branches:
   269→        remote_ref = f"origin/{branch_name}"
   270→        ctx.branch_manager.create_tracking_branch(repo.root, branch_name, remote_ref)
   271→
   272→    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, branch_name)
   273→
   274→    # Sync local branch with remote (may be behind from prior submission or CI)
   275→    pull_result = ctx.git.remote.pull_rebase(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)
   276→    if isinstance(pull_result, PullRebaseError):
   277→        raise UserFacingCliError(
   278→            f"Failed to sync branch '{branch_name}' with remote: {pull_result.message}"
   279→        )
   280→
   281→    # Clean up previous .erk/impl-context/ if it exists (e.g., from a prior failed dispatch)
   282→    if impl_context_exists(repo.root):
   283→        user_output("Cleaning up previous .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
   284→        remove_impl_context(repo.root)
   285→
   286→    # Create .erk/impl-context/ with planned-PR provider
   287→    user_output("Creating .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
   288→    create_impl_context(
   289→        plan_content=plan.body,
   290→        plan_id=str(plan_number),
   291→        url=validated.url,
   292→        repo_root=repo.root,
   293→        provider="github-draft-pr",
   294→        objective_id=plan.objective_id,
   295→        now_iso=ctx.time.now().isoformat(),
   296→    )
   297→
   298→    # Stage, commit, and push
   299→    ctx.git.commit.stage_files(repo.root, [IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR])
   300→    ctx.git.commit.commit(repo.root, f"Add plan for PR #{plan_number}")
   301→    push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
   302→        repo.root, "origin", branch_name, set_upstream=False, force=False
   303→    )
   304→    if isinstance(push_result, PushError):
   305→        raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message)
   306→    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Branch pushed to remote")
   307→
   308→    # Fix Graphite tracking divergence caused by pull_rebase + commit
   309→    if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None:
   310→        ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name)
   311→
   312→    # Switch back to original branch
   313→    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, original_branch)
   314→
   315→    # Gather submission metadata
   316→    queued_at = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
   317→
   318→    # Load workflow-specific config
   319→    workflow_config = load_workflow_config(repo.root, DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME)
   320→
   321→    # Build inputs dict with plan_backend="planned_pr"
   322→    user_output("")
   323→    user_output(f"Triggering workflow: {click.style(DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME, fg='cyan')}")
   324→
   325→    inputs = {
   326→        "plan_id": str(plan_number),
   327→        "submitted_by": submitted_by,
   328→        "plan_title": validated.title,
   329→        "branch_name": branch_name,
   330→        "pr_number": str(plan_number),
   331→        "base_branch": base_branch,
   332→        "plan_backend": "planned_pr",
   333→        **workflow_config,
   334→    }
   335→
   336→    run_id = ctx.github.trigger_workflow(
   337→        repo_root=repo.root,
   338→        workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME,
   339→        inputs=inputs,
   340→        ref=None,
   341→    )
   342→    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Workflow triggered.")
   343→
   344→    # Compute workflow URL
   345→    workflow_url = _build_workflow_run_url(validated.url, run_id)
   346→
   347→    # Write dispatch metadata FIRST (before any PR body modification)
   348→    try:
   349→        write_dispatch_metadata(
   350→            plan_backend=ctx.plan_backend,
   351→            github=ctx.github,
   352→            repo_root=repo.root,
   353→            plan_number=plan_number,
   354→            run_id=run_id,
   355→            dispatched_at=queued_at,
   356→        )
   357→        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Dispatch metadata written")
   358→    except Exception as e:
   359→        user_output(
   360→            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Failed to update dispatch metadata: {e}"
   361→        )
   362→
   363→    # Update PR body with workflow run link (best-effort)
   364→    # Guard: only append if body is non-empty to avoid overwriting metadata block
   365→    try:
   366→        pr_details = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
   367→        if not isinstance(pr_details, PRNotFound) and pr_details.body:
   368→            updated_body = pr_details.body + f"\n\n**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
   369→            ctx.github.update_pr_body(repo.root, plan_number, updated_body)
   370→    except Exception as e:
   371→        logger.warning("Failed to update PR body with workflow run link: %s", e)
   372→
   373→    # Post queued event comment via PlanBackend
   374→    try:
   375→        validation_results = {
   376→            "pr_is_open": True,
   377→            "has_erk_plan_label": True,
   378→        }
   379→
   380→        metadata_block = create_submission_queued_block(
   381→            queued_at=queued_at,
   382→            submitted_by=submitted_by,
   383→            issue_number=plan_number,
   384→            validation_results=validation_results,
   385→            expected_workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME,
   386→        )
   387→
   388→        comment_body = render_erk_issue_event(
   389→            title="Plan Queued for Implementation",
   390→            metadata=metadata_block,
   391→            description=(
   392→                f"Plan submitted by **{submitted_by}** at {queued_at}.\n\n"
   393→                f"The `{DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME}` workflow has been "
   394→                f"triggered via direct dispatch.\n\n"
   395→                f"**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
   396→            ),
   397→        )
   398→
   399→        user_output("Posting queued event comment...")
   400→        ctx.plan_backend.add_comment(repo.root, str(plan_number), comment_body)
   401→        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Queued event comment posted")
   402→    except Exception as e:
   403→        user_output(
   404→            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")
   405→            + f"Failed to post queued comment: {e}\n"
   406→            + "Workflow is already running."
   407→        )
   408→
   409→    pr_url = _build_pr_url(validated.url, plan_number)
   410→
   411→    return DispatchResult(
   412→        plan_number=plan_number,
   413→        plan_title=validated.title,
   414→        plan_url=validated.url,
   415→        pr_number=plan_number,
   416→        pr_url=pr_url,
   417→        workflow_run_id=run_id,
   418→        workflow_url=workflow_url,
   419→    )
   420→
   421→
   422→def _detect_plan_number_from_context(
   423→    repo: RepoContext,
   424→) -> int | None:
   425→    """Detect plan PR number from local context when no argument given.
   426→
   427→    Fallback chain:
   428→    1. .impl/plan-ref.json (or ref.json, issue.json) — local impl folder
   429→    2. .erk/impl-context/ref.json — committed staging directory
   430→
   431→    Args:
   432→        repo: Repository context
   433→
   434→    Returns:
   435→        Detected PR number, or None if nothing found.
   436→    """
   437→    # 1. Check .impl/ folder
   438→    impl_dir = repo.root / ".impl"
   439→    if impl_dir.exists():
   440→        plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
   441→        if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
   442→            return int(plan_ref.plan_id)
   443→
   444→    # 2. Check .erk/impl-context/
   445→    impl_context_dir = repo.root / IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR
   446→    if impl_context_dir.exists():
   447→        plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_context_dir)
   448→        if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
   449→            return int(plan_ref.plan_id)
   450→
   451→    return None
   452→
   453→
   454→@click.command("dispatch")
   455→@click.argument("plan_numbers", type=int, nargs=-1, required=False)
   456→@click.option(
   457→    "--base",
   458→    type=str,
   459→    default=None,
   460→    help="Base branch for PR (defaults to current branch).",
   461→)
   462→@click.pass_obj
   463→def pr_dispatch(ctx: ErkContext, plan_numbers: tuple[int, ...], base: str | None) -> None:
   464→    """Dispatch plans for remote AI implementation via GitHub Actions.
   465→
   466→    Creates branch and draft PR locally (for correct commit attribution),
   467→    then triggers the plan-implement.yml GitHub Actions workflow.
   468→
   469→    Arguments:
   470→        PLAN_NUMBERS: One or more plan numbers to dispatch.
   471→            If omitted, auto-detects from .impl/, .erk/impl-context/, or current branch.
   472→
   473→    \b
   474→    Example:
   475→        erk pr dispatch 123
   476→        erk pr dispatch 123 456 789
   477→        erk pr dispatch 123 --base master
   478→        erk pr dispatch                     # auto-detect from context
   479→
   480→    Requires:
   481→        - All issues must have erk-plan label
   482→        - All issues must be OPEN
   483→        - Working directory must be clean (no uncommitted changes)
   484→    """
   485→    # Validate GitHub CLI prerequisites upfront (LBYL)
   486→    user_output("Checking GitHub authentication...")
   487→    Ensure.gh_authenticated(ctx)
   488→
   489→    # Get repository context
   490→    if isinstance(ctx.repo, RepoContext):
   491→        repo = ctx.repo
   492→    else:
   493→        repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
   494→
   495→    # Ensure trunk is synced before any operations
   496→    user_output("Syncing trunk with remote...")
   497→    ensure_trunk_synced(ctx, repo)
   498→
   499→    # Save current state (needed for both default base and restoration)
   500→    original_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(repo.root)
   501→    if original_branch is None:
   502→        user_output(
   503→            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   504→            + "Not on a branch (detached HEAD state). Cannot dispatch from here."
   505→        )
   506→        raise SystemExit(1)
   507→
   508→    # If no arguments given, try to auto-detect from context
   509→    if not plan_numbers:
   510→        detected = _detect_plan_number_from_context(repo)
   511→        if detected is None:
   512→            user_output(
   513→                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
   514→                + "No plan numbers provided and could not auto-detect from context.\n\n"
   515→                "Provide plan numbers explicitly: erk pr dispatch <number>\n"
   516→                "Or run from a plan branch with an associated PR."
   517→            )
   518→            raise SystemExit(1)
   519→        user_output(f"Auto-detected PR #{detected} from context")
   520→        plan_numbers = (detected,)
   521→
   522→    # Validate base branch if provided, otherwise default to current branch (LBYL)
   523→    if base is not None:
   524→        if not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", base):
   525→            user_output(
   526→                click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Base branch '{base}' does not exist on remote"
   527→            )
   528→            raise SystemExit(1)
   529→        target_branch = base
   530→    else:
   531→        # If on a placeholder branch (local-only), use trunk as base
   532→        if is_placeholder_branch(original_branch):
   533→            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
   534→        elif not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", original_branch):
   535→            # Current branch not pushed to remote - fall back to trunk
   536→            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
   537→        else:
   538→            target_branch = original_branch
   539→
   540→    # For single-plan learn plan dispatches, auto-detect parent branch
   541→    plan_number = plan_numbers[0] if len(plan_numbers) == 1 else None
   542→    if plan_number is not None and base is None:
   543→        user_output(f"Checking plan #{plan_number}...")
   544→        if ctx.issues.issue_exists(repo.root, plan_number):
   545→            issue = ctx.issues.get_issue(repo.root, plan_number)
   546→            # issue_exists check above ensures this won't be IssueNotFound
   547→            if not isinstance(issue, IssueNotFound) and is_issue_learn_plan(issue.labels):
   548→                parent_branch = get_learn_plan_parent_branch(ctx, repo.root, str(plan_number))
   549→                if parent_branch is not None and ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(
   550→                    repo.root, "origin", parent_branch
   551→                ):
   552→                    target_branch = parent_branch
   553→                    user_output(
   554→                        f"Learn plan detected, stacking on parent branch: "
   555→                        f"{click.style(parent_branch, fg='cyan')}"
   556→                    )
   557→                elif parent_branch is not None:
   558→                    user_output(
   559→                        click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")
   560→                        + f"Parent branch '{parent_branch}' not on remote, using trunk"
   561→                    )
   562→
   563→    # Get GitHub username (authentication already validated)
   564→    user_output("Resolving GitHub username...")
   565→    _, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
   566→    submitted_by = username or "unknown"
   567→
   568→    # Validate all planned-PR plans upfront
   569→    user_output(f"Validating {len(plan_numbers)} planned-PR plan(s)...")
   570→    user_output("")
   571→
   572→    validated_planned_prs: list[ValidatedPlannedPR] = []
   573→    for plan_number in plan_numbers:
   574→        user_output(f"Validating PR #{plan_number}...")
   575→        validated_pr = _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(ctx, repo, plan_number)
   576→        validated_planned_prs.append(validated_pr)
   577→
   578→    user_output("")
   579→    user_output(
   580→        click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" All {len(validated_planned_prs)} plan(s) validated"
   581→    )
   582→    user_output("")
   583→
   584→    for v in validated_planned_prs:
   585→        user_output(f"  #{v.number}: {click.style(v.title, fg='yellow')}")
   586→    user_output("")
   587→
   588→    # Dispatch all validated plans
   589→    results: list[DispatchResult] = []
   590→    for i, v in enumerate(validated_planned_prs):
   591→        if len(validated_planned_prs) > 1:
   592→            count = len(validated_planned_prs)
   593→            user_output(f"--- Dispatching PR {i + 1}/{count}: #{v.number} ---")
   594→        else:
   595→            user_output(f"Dispatching PR #{v.number}...")
   596→        user_output("")
   597→        result = _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
   598→            ctx,
   599→            repo=repo,
   600→            validated=v,
   601→            submitted_by=submitted_by,
   602→            original_branch=original_branch,
   603→            base_branch=target_branch,
   604→        )
   605→        results.append(result)
   606→        user_output("")
   607→
   608→    # Success output
   609→    user_output("")
   610→    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" {len(results)} plan(s) dispatched successfully!")
   611→    user_output("")
   612→    user_output("Dispatched plans:")
   613→    for r in results:
   614→        user_output(f"  #{r.plan_number}: {r.plan_title}")
   615→        user_output(f"    Plan: {r.plan_url}")
   616→        if r.pr_url:
   617→            user_output(f"    PR: {r.pr_url}")
   618→        user_output(f"    Workflow: {r.workflow_url}")
   619→

"""Dispatch plans for remote AI implementation via GitHub Actions."""

import logging
import tomllib
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import UTC, datetime
from pathlib import Path

import click

from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced
from erk.cli.commands.pr.metadata_helpers import write_dispatch_metadata
from erk.cli.commands.slot.common import is_placeholder_branch
from erk.cli.constants import (
    DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME,
    DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME,
    ERK_PLAN_LABEL,
)
from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure, UserFacingCliError
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext
from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PullRebaseError, PushError
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound
from erk_shared.gateway.github.metadata.core import (
    create_submission_queued_block,
    render_erk_issue_event,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import (
    construct_pr_url,
    construct_workflow_run_url,
    extract_owner_repo_from_github_url,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRNotFound
from erk_shared.impl_context import (
    create_impl_context,
    impl_context_exists,
    remove_impl_context,
)
from erk_shared.impl_folder import read_plan_ref
from erk_shared.output.output import user_output
from erk_shared.plan_store.planned_pr_lifecycle import IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR
from erk_shared.plan_store.types import PlanNotFound

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def is_issue_learn_plan(labels: list[str]) -> bool:
    """Check if an issue is a learn plan by checking for erk-learn label.

    Args:
        labels: The issue's labels

    Returns:
        True if the issue has the erk-learn label, False otherwise
    """
    return "erk-learn" in labels


def get_learn_plan_parent_branch(ctx: ErkContext, repo_root: Path, plan_id: str) -> str | None:
    """Get the parent branch for a learn plan.

    Learn plans should stack on their parent plan's branch.
    Extracts learned_from_issue via plan_backend, fetches parent's branch_name.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with plan_backend
        repo_root: Repository root path
        plan_id: Plan identifier

    Returns:
        Parent plan's branch_name if found, None otherwise
    """
    learned_from = ctx.plan_backend.get_metadata_field(repo_root, plan_id, "learned_from_issue")
    if isinstance(learned_from, PlanNotFound) or learned_from is None:
        return None

    branch_name = ctx.plan_backend.get_metadata_field(repo_root, str(learned_from), "branch_name")
    if isinstance(branch_name, PlanNotFound) or branch_name is None:
        return None
    return str(branch_name)


def load_workflow_config(repo_root: Path, workflow_name: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    """Load workflow config from .erk/config.toml [workflows.<name>] section.

    Args:
        repo_root: Repository root path
        workflow_name: Workflow filename (with or without .yml/.yaml extension).
            Only the basename is used for config lookup.

    Returns:
        Dict of string key-value pairs for workflow inputs.
        Returns empty dict if config file or section doesn't exist.

    Example:
        For workflow_name="plan-implement.yml", reads from:
        .erk/config.toml -> [workflows.plan-implement] section
    """
    config_path = repo_root / ".erk" / "config.toml"

    if not config_path.exists():
        return {}

    with open(config_path, "rb") as f:
        data = tomllib.load(f)

    # Extract basename and strip .yml/.yaml extension
    basename = Path(workflow_name).name
    config_name = basename.removesuffix(".yml").removesuffix(".yaml")

    # Get [workflows.<name>] section
    workflows_section = data.get("workflows", {})
    workflow_config = workflows_section.get(config_name, {})

    # Convert all values to strings (workflow inputs are always strings)
    return {k: str(v) for k, v in workflow_config.items()}


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DispatchResult:
    """Result of dispatching a single plan."""

    plan_number: int
    plan_title: str
    plan_url: str
    pr_number: int | None
    pr_url: str | None
    workflow_run_id: str
    workflow_url: str


def _build_workflow_run_url(plan_url: str, run_id: str) -> str:
    """Construct GitHub Actions workflow run URL from plan URL and run ID.

    Args:
        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
        run_id: Workflow run ID

    Returns:
        Workflow run URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/1234567890)
    """
    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
    if owner_repo is not None:
        owner, repo = owner_repo
        return construct_workflow_run_url(owner, repo, run_id)
    return f"https://github.com/actions/runs/{run_id}"


def _build_pr_url(plan_url: str, pr_number: int) -> str:
    """Construct GitHub PR URL from plan URL and PR number.

    Args:
        plan_url: GitHub plan URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123)
        pr_number: PR number

    Returns:
        PR URL (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/456)
    """
    owner_repo = extract_owner_repo_from_github_url(plan_url)
    if owner_repo is not None:
        owner, repo = owner_repo
        return construct_pr_url(owner, repo, pr_number)
    return f"https://github.com/pull/{pr_number}"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ValidatedPlannedPR:
    """Draft PR that passed all validation checks."""

    number: int
    title: str
    url: str
    branch_name: str


def _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    repo: RepoContext,
    plan_number: int,
) -> ValidatedPlannedPR:
    """Validate a planned PR plan for dispatch.

    Fetches the PR, validates it has the erk-plan label and is OPEN.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
        repo: Repository context
        plan_number: PR number to validate

    Raises:
        SystemExit: If PR doesn't exist, missing label, or not OPEN.
    """
    pr_result = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
    if isinstance(pr_result, PRNotFound):
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} not found")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # Validate: must have erk-plan label
    if ERK_PLAN_LABEL not in pr_result.labels:
        user_output(
            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
            + f"PR #{plan_number} does not have {ERK_PLAN_LABEL} label\n\n"
            "Cannot dispatch non-plan PRs for automated implementation."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # Validate: must be OPEN
    if pr_result.state != "OPEN":
        user_output(
            click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number} is {pr_result.state}\n\n"
            "Cannot dispatch closed PRs for automated implementation."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    return ValidatedPlannedPR(
        number=plan_number,
        title=pr_result.title,
        url=pr_result.url,
        branch_name=pr_result.head_ref_name,
    )


def _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    repo: RepoContext,
    validated: ValidatedPlannedPR,
    submitted_by: str,
    original_branch: str,
    base_branch: str,
) -> DispatchResult:
    """Dispatch a validated planned-PR plan for implementation.

    For planned-PR plans, the branch and PR already exist. This function:
    - Fetches and checks out the existing plan branch
    - Creates .erk/impl-context/ with provider="github-draft-pr"
    - Commits and pushes .erk/impl-context/ to existing branch
    - Triggers the workflow with plan_backend="planned_pr"

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with git operations
        repo: Repository context
        validated: Validated planned PR information
        submitted_by: GitHub username of submitter
        original_branch: Original branch name (to restore after)
        base_branch: Base branch for PR

    Returns:
        DispatchResult with URLs and identifiers.
    """
    plan_number = validated.number
    branch_name = validated.branch_name

    # Fetch plan content via PlanBackend
    user_output("Fetching plan content...")
    result = ctx.plan_store.get_plan(repo.root, str(plan_number))
    if isinstance(result, PlanNotFound):
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"PR #{plan_number}: plan content not found")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    plan = result

    # Fetch and checkout the existing plan branch
    user_output(f"Checking out existing plan branch: {click.style(branch_name, fg='cyan')}")
    ctx.git.remote.fetch_branch(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)

    local_branches = ctx.git.branch.list_local_branches(repo.root)
    if branch_name not in local_branches:
        remote_ref = f"origin/{branch_name}"
        ctx.branch_manager.create_tracking_branch(repo.root, branch_name, remote_ref)

    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, branch_name)

    # Sync local branch with remote (may be behind from prior submission or CI)
    pull_result = ctx.git.remote.pull_rebase(repo.root, "origin", branch_name)
    if isinstance(pull_result, PullRebaseError):
        raise UserFacingCliError(
            f"Failed to sync branch '{branch_name}' with remote: {pull_result.message}"
        )

    # Clean up previous .erk/impl-context/ if it exists (e.g., from a prior failed dispatch)
    if impl_context_exists(repo.root):
        user_output("Cleaning up previous .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
        remove_impl_context(repo.root)

    # Create .erk/impl-context/ with planned-PR provider
    user_output("Creating .erk/impl-context/ folder...")
    create_impl_context(
        plan_content=plan.body,
        plan_id=str(plan_number),
        url=validated.url,
        repo_root=repo.root,
        provider="github-draft-pr",
        objective_id=plan.objective_id,
        now_iso=ctx.time.now().isoformat(),
    )

    # Stage, commit, and push
    ctx.git.commit.stage_files(repo.root, [IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR])
    ctx.git.commit.commit(repo.root, f"Add plan for PR #{plan_number}")
    push_result = ctx.git.remote.push_to_remote(
        repo.root, "origin", branch_name, set_upstream=False, force=False
    )
    if isinstance(push_result, PushError):
        raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message)
    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Branch pushed to remote")

    # Fix Graphite tracking divergence caused by pull_rebase + commit
    if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None:
        ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(repo.root, branch_name)

    # Switch back to original branch
    ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(repo.root, original_branch)

    # Gather submission metadata
    queued_at = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()

    # Load workflow-specific config
    workflow_config = load_workflow_config(repo.root, DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME)

    # Build inputs dict with plan_backend="planned_pr"
    user_output("")
    user_output(f"Triggering workflow: {click.style(DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME, fg='cyan')}")

    inputs = {
        "plan_id": str(plan_number),
        "submitted_by": submitted_by,
        "plan_title": validated.title,
        "branch_name": branch_name,
        "pr_number": str(plan_number),
        "base_branch": base_branch,
        "plan_backend": "planned_pr",
        **workflow_config,
    }

    run_id = ctx.github.trigger_workflow(
        repo_root=repo.root,
        workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME,
        inputs=inputs,
        ref=None,
    )
    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Workflow triggered.")

    # Compute workflow URL
    workflow_url = _build_workflow_run_url(validated.url, run_id)

    # Write dispatch metadata FIRST (before any PR body modification)
    try:
        write_dispatch_metadata(
            plan_backend=ctx.plan_backend,
            github=ctx.github,
            repo_root=repo.root,
            plan_number=plan_number,
            run_id=run_id,
            dispatched_at=queued_at,
        )
        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Dispatch metadata written")
    except Exception as e:
        user_output(
            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + f"Failed to update dispatch metadata: {e}"
        )

    # Update PR body with workflow run link (best-effort)
    # Guard: only append if body is non-empty to avoid overwriting metadata block
    try:
        pr_details = ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, plan_number)
        if not isinstance(pr_details, PRNotFound) and pr_details.body:
            updated_body = pr_details.body + f"\n\n**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
            ctx.github.update_pr_body(repo.root, plan_number, updated_body)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.warning("Failed to update PR body with workflow run link: %s", e)

    # Post queued event comment via PlanBackend
    try:
        validation_results = {
            "pr_is_open": True,
            "has_erk_plan_label": True,
        }

        metadata_block = create_submission_queued_block(
            queued_at=queued_at,
            submitted_by=submitted_by,
            issue_number=plan_number,
            validation_results=validation_results,
            expected_workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME,
        )

        comment_body = render_erk_issue_event(
            title="Plan Queued for Implementation",
            metadata=metadata_block,
            description=(
                f"Plan submitted by **{submitted_by}** at {queued_at}.\n\n"
                f"The `{DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_METADATA_NAME}` workflow has been "
                f"triggered via direct dispatch.\n\n"
                f"**Workflow run:** {workflow_url}"
            ),
        )

        user_output("Posting queued event comment...")
        ctx.plan_backend.add_comment(repo.root, str(plan_number), comment_body)
        user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + " Queued event comment posted")
    except Exception as e:
        user_output(
            click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")
            + f"Failed to post queued comment: {e}\n"
            + "Workflow is already running."
        )

    pr_url = _build_pr_url(validated.url, plan_number)

    return DispatchResult(
        plan_number=plan_number,
        plan_title=validated.title,
        plan_url=validated.url,
        pr_number=plan_number,
        pr_url=pr_url,
        workflow_run_id=run_id,
        workflow_url=workflow_url,
    )


def _detect_plan_number_from_context(
    repo: RepoContext,
) -> int | None:
    """Detect plan PR number from local context when no argument given.

    Fallback chain:
    1. .impl/plan-ref.json (or ref.json, issue.json) — local impl folder
    2. .erk/impl-context/ref.json — committed staging directory

    Args:
        repo: Repository context

    Returns:
        Detected PR number, or None if nothing found.
    """
    # 1. Check .impl/ folder
    impl_dir = repo.root / ".impl"
    if impl_dir.exists():
        plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_dir)
        if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
            return int(plan_ref.plan_id)

    # 2. Check .erk/impl-context/
    impl_context_dir = repo.root / IMPL_CONTEXT_DIR
    if impl_context_dir.exists():
        plan_ref = read_plan_ref(impl_context_dir)
        if plan_ref is not None and plan_ref.plan_id.isdigit():
            return int(plan_ref.plan_id)

    return None


@click.command("dispatch")
@click.argument("plan_numbers", type=int, nargs=-1, required=False)
@click.option(
    "--base",
    type=str,
    default=None,
    help="Base branch for PR (defaults to current branch).",
)
@click.pass_obj
def pr_dispatch(ctx: ErkContext, plan_numbers: tuple[int, ...], base: str | None) -> None:
    """Dispatch plans for remote AI implementation via GitHub Actions.

    Creates branch and draft PR locally (for correct commit attribution),
    then triggers the plan-implement.yml GitHub Actions workflow.

    Arguments:
        PLAN_NUMBERS: One or more plan numbers to dispatch.
            If omitted, auto-detects from .impl/, .erk/impl-context/, or current branch.

    \b
    Example:
        erk pr dispatch 123
        erk pr dispatch 123 456 789
        erk pr dispatch 123 --base master
        erk pr dispatch                     # auto-detect from context

    Requires:
        - All issues must have erk-plan label
        - All issues must be OPEN
        - Working directory must be clean (no uncommitted changes)
    """
    # Validate GitHub CLI prerequisites upfront (LBYL)
    user_output("Checking GitHub authentication...")
    Ensure.gh_authenticated(ctx)

    # Get repository context
    if isinstance(ctx.repo, RepoContext):
        repo = ctx.repo
    else:
        repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)

    # Ensure trunk is synced before any operations
    user_output("Syncing trunk with remote...")
    ensure_trunk_synced(ctx, repo)

    # Save current state (needed for both default base and restoration)
    original_branch = ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(repo.root)
    if original_branch is None:
        user_output(
            click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
            + "Not on a branch (detached HEAD state). Cannot dispatch from here."
        )
        raise SystemExit(1)

    # If no arguments given, try to auto-detect from context
    if not plan_numbers:
        detected = _detect_plan_number_from_context(repo)
        if detected is None:
            user_output(
                click.style("Error: ", fg="red")
                + "No plan numbers provided and could not auto-detect from context.\n\n"
                "Provide plan numbers explicitly: erk pr dispatch <number>\n"
                "Or run from a plan branch with an associated PR."
            )
            raise SystemExit(1)
        user_output(f"Auto-detected PR #{detected} from context")
        plan_numbers = (detected,)

    # Validate base branch if provided, otherwise default to current branch (LBYL)
    if base is not None:
        if not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", base):
            user_output(
                click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + f"Base branch '{base}' does not exist on remote"
            )
            raise SystemExit(1)
        target_branch = base
    else:
        # If on a placeholder branch (local-only), use trunk as base
        if is_placeholder_branch(original_branch):
            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
        elif not ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(repo.root, "origin", original_branch):
            # Current branch not pushed to remote - fall back to trunk
            target_branch = ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root)
        else:
            target_branch = original_branch

    # For single-plan learn plan dispatches, auto-detect parent branch
    plan_number = plan_numbers[0] if len(plan_numbers) == 1 else None
    if plan_number is not None and base is None:
        user_output(f"Checking plan #{plan_number}...")
        if ctx.issues.issue_exists(repo.root, plan_number):
            issue = ctx.issues.get_issue(repo.root, plan_number)
            # issue_exists check above ensures this won't be IssueNotFound
            if not isinstance(issue, IssueNotFound) and is_issue_learn_plan(issue.labels):
                parent_branch = get_learn_plan_parent_branch(ctx, repo.root, str(plan_number))
                if parent_branch is not None and ctx.git.branch.branch_exists_on_remote(
                    repo.root, "origin", parent_branch
                ):
                    target_branch = parent_branch
                    user_output(
                        f"Learn plan detected, stacking on parent branch: "
                        f"{click.style(parent_branch, fg='cyan')}"
                    )
                elif parent_branch is not None:
                    user_output(
                        click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow")
                        + f"Parent branch '{parent_branch}' not on remote, using trunk"
                    )

    # Get GitHub username (authentication already validated)
    user_output("Resolving GitHub username...")
    _, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
    submitted_by = username or "unknown"

    # Validate all planned-PR plans upfront
    user_output(f"Validating {len(plan_numbers)} planned-PR plan(s)...")
    user_output("")

    validated_planned_prs: list[ValidatedPlannedPR] = []
    for plan_number in plan_numbers:
        user_output(f"Validating PR #{plan_number}...")
        validated_pr = _validate_planned_pr_for_dispatch(ctx, repo, plan_number)
        validated_planned_prs.append(validated_pr)

    user_output("")
    user_output(
        click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" All {len(validated_planned_prs)} plan(s) validated"
    )
    user_output("")

    for v in validated_planned_prs:
        user_output(f"  #{v.number}: {click.style(v.title, fg='yellow')}")
    user_output("")

    # Dispatch all validated plans
    results: list[DispatchResult] = []
    for i, v in enumerate(validated_planned_prs):
        if len(validated_planned_prs) > 1:
            count = len(validated_planned_prs)
            user_output(f"--- Dispatching PR {i + 1}/{count}: #{v.number} ---")
        else:
            user_output(f"Dispatching PR #{v.number}...")
        user_output("")
        result = _dispatch_planned_pr_plan(
            ctx,
            repo=repo,
            validated=v,
            submitted_by=submitted_by,
            original_branch=original_branch,
            base_branch=target_branch,
        )
        results.append(result)
        user_output("")

    # Success output
    user_output("")
    user_output(click.style("✓", fg="green") + f" {len(results)} plan(s) dispatched successfully!")
    user_output("")
    user_output("Dispatched plans:")
    for r in results:
        user_output(f"  #{r.plan_number}: {r.plan_title}")
        user_output(f"    Plan: {r.plan_url}")
        if r.pr_url:
            user_output(f"    PR: {r.pr_url}")
        user_output(f"    Workflow: {r.workflow_url}")

"""Squash, regenerate AI commit message, push, and update remote PR.

Replaces the old multi-step workflow (gt squash → erk pr summarize → push)
with a single command that handles the full cycle: squash commits, generate
an AI-powered commit message, amend the local commit, force-push, and update
the remote PR title/body.
"""

from pathlib import Path

import click

from erk.cli.commands.pr.shared import (
    assemble_pr_body,
    cleanup_diff_file,
    discover_branch_context,
    echo_plan_context_status,
    maybe_advance_lifecycle_to_impl,
    render_progress,
    require_claude_available,
    run_commit_message_generation,
    run_diff_extraction,
)
from erk.core.command_log import get_or_generate_session_id
from erk.core.commit_message_generator import CommitMessageGenerator
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.plan_context_provider import PlanContextProvider
from erk_shared.gateway.branch_manager.types import SubmitBranchError
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import BodyText, PRNotFound
from erk_shared.gateway.gt.events import CompletionEvent, ProgressEvent
from erk_shared.gateway.gt.operations.finalize import ERK_SKIP_LEARN_LABEL, is_learn_plan
from erk_shared.gateway.gt.operations.squash import execute_squash
from erk_shared.gateway.gt.types import SquashError


@click.command("rewrite")
@click.option("--debug", is_flag=True, help="Show diagnostic output")
@click.pass_obj
def pr_rewrite(ctx: ErkContext, debug: bool) -> None:
    """Squash, regenerate AI commit message, push, and update remote PR.

    Combines squashing, AI message generation, amending, pushing, and
    PR updating into a single command. Replaces the manual workflow of
    running gt squash, erk pr summarize, and pushing separately.

    \b
    Phases:
      1. Validate preconditions (branch, PR)
      2. Squash commits (idempotent)
      3. Extract diff
      4. Generate AI title/body
      5. Amend local commit
      6. Push and update remote PR

    Examples:

    \b
      # Rewrite current PR with AI-generated message
      erk pr rewrite

      # Show debug output
      erk pr rewrite --debug
    """
    _execute_pr_rewrite(ctx, debug=debug)


def _execute_pr_rewrite(ctx: ErkContext, *, debug: bool) -> None:
    """Execute PR rewrite with positively-named parameters."""
    # Phase 1: Validate preconditions
    require_claude_available(ctx)

    cwd = Path.cwd()
    session_id = get_or_generate_session_id(cwd)

    discovery = discover_branch_context(ctx, cwd=cwd)

    # Verify PR exists for this branch
    pr_info = ctx.github.get_pr_for_branch(discovery.repo_root, discovery.current_branch)
    if isinstance(pr_info, PRNotFound):
        raise click.ClickException(
            f"No PR found for branch '{discovery.current_branch}'\n\n"
            "Create a PR first with: erk pr submit"
        )

    pr_number = pr_info.number

    click.echo(click.style("📝 Rewriting PR title and description...", bold=True))
    click.echo("")

    # Phase 2: Squash commits (idempotent)
    click.echo(click.style("Phase 1: Squashing commits", bold=True))
    squash_result = _run_squash(ctx, cwd=cwd, debug=debug)
    if isinstance(squash_result, SquashError):
        raise click.ClickException(f"Squash failed: {squash_result.message}")
    click.echo("")

    # Phase 3: Extract diff
    click.echo(click.style("Phase 2: Getting diff", bold=True))
    diff_file = run_diff_extraction(
        ctx,
        cwd=cwd,
        session_id=session_id,
        base_branch=discovery.parent_branch,
        debug=debug,
    )
    if diff_file is None:
        raise click.ClickException("Failed to extract diff for AI analysis")
    click.echo("")

    # Phase 4: Generate AI title/body
    click.echo(click.style("Phase 3: Generating commit message", bold=True))

    plan_provider = PlanContextProvider(
        plan_backend=ctx.plan_backend, github_issues=ctx.github_issues
    )
    plan_context = plan_provider.get_plan_context(
        repo_root=discovery.repo_root,
        branch_name=discovery.current_branch,
    )

    echo_plan_context_status(plan_context)

    msg_gen = CommitMessageGenerator(ctx.prompt_executor)
    msg_result = run_commit_message_generation(
        generator=msg_gen,
        diff_file=diff_file,
        repo_root=discovery.repo_root,
        current_branch=discovery.current_branch,
        parent_branch=discovery.parent_branch,
        commit_messages=None,
        plan_context=plan_context,
        debug=debug,
    )

    if not msg_result.success:
        raise click.ClickException(f"Failed to generate message: {msg_result.error_message}")

    title = msg_result.title or "Update"
    body = msg_result.body or ""
    click.echo("")

    # Phase 5: Amend local commit
    click.echo(click.style("Phase 4: Amending commit", bold=True))
    commit_message = f"{title}\n\n{body}" if body else title
    ctx.git.commit.amend_commit(cwd, commit_message)
    click.echo(click.style("   Commit amended", fg="green"))
    click.echo("")

    # Phase 6: Push and update remote PR
    click.echo(click.style("Phase 5: Pushing and updating PR", bold=True))

    submit_result = ctx.branch_manager.submit_branch(discovery.repo_root, discovery.current_branch)
    if isinstance(submit_result, SubmitBranchError):
        raise click.ClickException(f"Push failed: {submit_result.message}")
    click.echo(click.style("   Branch pushed", fg="green"))

    impl_dir = cwd / ".impl"

    final_body = assemble_pr_body(
        body=body,
        plan_context=plan_context,
        pr_number=pr_number,
        header="",
        existing_pr_body=pr_info.body,
    )

    ctx.github.update_pr_title_and_body(
        repo_root=discovery.repo_root,
        pr_number=pr_number,
        title=title,
        body=BodyText(content=final_body),
    )
    click.echo(click.style("   PR updated", fg="green"))

    # Update lifecycle stage for linked plan
    if plan_context is not None:
        maybe_advance_lifecycle_to_impl(
            ctx,
            repo_root=discovery.repo_root,
            plan_id=plan_context.plan_id,
            quiet=False,
        )

    # Add learn skip label if applicable
    is_learn_origin = is_learn_plan(impl_dir)
    if is_learn_origin:
        ctx.github.add_label_to_pr(discovery.repo_root, pr_number, ERK_SKIP_LEARN_LABEL)

    # Retrack Graphite branch if needed (fix tracking divergence from amend)
    if ctx.graphite_branch_ops is not None:
        ctx.graphite_branch_ops.retrack_branch(discovery.repo_root, discovery.current_branch)

    # Clean up scratch diff file
    cleanup_diff_file(diff_file)

    click.echo("")
    click.echo(f"✅ PR title and description updated: {title}")


def _run_squash(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    cwd: Path,
    debug: bool,
) -> SquashError | None:
    """Run squash phase. Returns SquashError on failure, None on success."""
    for event in execute_squash(ctx, cwd):
        if isinstance(event, ProgressEvent):
            if debug:
                render_progress(event)
        elif isinstance(event, CompletionEvent):
            result = event.result
            if isinstance(result, SquashError):
                return result
    return None

    20→class SlotAllocationResult:
    21→    """Result of allocating a slot for a branch."""
    22→

    28→# Default pool configuration

    32→
    33→def extract_slot_number(slot_name: str) -> str | None:
    34→    """Extract slot number from slot name.
    35→
    36→    Args:
    37→        slot_name: Slot name like "erk-slot-03"
    38→
    39→    Returns:
    40→        Two-digit slot number (e.g., "03") or None if not in expected format
    41→    """
    42→    if not slot_name.startswith(SLOT_NAME_PREFIX + "-"):
    43→        return None
    44→    suffix = slot_name[len(SLOT_NAME_PREFIX) + 1 :]
    45→    if len(suffix) != 2 or not suffix.isdigit():
    46→        return None
    47→    return suffix

    49→
    50→def get_placeholder_branch_name(slot_name: str) -> str | None:
    51→    """Get placeholder branch name for a slot.
    52→
    53→    Args:
    54→        slot_name: Slot name like "erk-slot-03"
    55→
    56→    Returns:
    57→        Placeholder branch name like "__erk-slot-03-br-stub__",
    58→        or None if slot_name is not in expected format
    59→    """
    60→    slot_number = extract_slot_number(slot_name)
    61→    if slot_number is None:
    62→        return None
    63→    return f"__erk-slot-{slot_number}-br-stub__"

    65→
    66→def is_placeholder_branch(branch_name: str) -> bool:
    67→    """Check if a branch name is an erk slot placeholder branch.
    68→
    69→    Placeholder branches have the format: __erk-slot-XX-br-stub__
    70→
    71→    Args:

    74→    Returns:
    75→        True if branch_name matches the placeholder pattern

    77→    return bool(re.match(r"^__erk-slot-\d+-br-stub__$", branch_name))

    80→def get_pool_size(ctx: ErkContext) -> int:

    94→def generate_slot_name(slot_number: int) -> str:
    95→    """Generate a slot name from a slot number.
    96→
    97→    Args:
    98→        slot_number: 1-based slot number
    99→
   100→    Returns:
   101→        Formatted slot name like "erk-slot-01"

   106→def find_next_available_slot(state: PoolState, worktrees_dir: Path | None) -> int | None:
   107→    """Find the next available slot number for on-demand worktree creation.
   108→
   109→    This function finds a slot number that is:
   110→    1. Not currently assigned to a branch (not in state.assignments)
   111→    2. Not already initialized as a worktree (not in state.slots)
   112→    3. Does not have an orphaned directory on disk (if worktrees_dir provided)
   113→
   114→    This ensures on-demand creation only targets slots where no worktree
   115→    exists on disk.
   116→
   117→    Args:
   118→        state: Current pool state
   119→        worktrees_dir: Directory containing worktrees, or None to skip disk check
   120→
   121→    Returns:
   122→        1-based slot number if available, None if pool is full
   123→    """
   124→    assigned_slots = {a.slot_name for a in state.assignments}
   125→    initialized_slots = {s.name for s in state.slots}
   126→
   127→    for slot_num in range(1, state.pool_size + 1):
   128→        slot_name = generate_slot_name(slot_num)
   129→        if slot_name not in assigned_slots and slot_name not in initialized_slots:
   130→            # Check if directory exists on disk (orphaned worktree)
   131→            if worktrees_dir is not None:
   132→                slot_path = worktrees_dir / slot_name
   133→                if slot_path.exists():
   134→                    continue  # Skip - directory exists but not tracked
   135→            return slot_num
   136→
   137→    return None
   138→
   139→
   140→def find_inactive_slot(
   141→    state: PoolState,
   142→    git: Git,
   143→    repo_root: Path,
   144→) -> tuple[str, Path] | None:
   145→    """Find an available managed slot for reuse.
   146→
   147→    Searches for worktrees that exist but are not assigned.
   148→    Uses git as source of truth for which worktrees exist.
   149→    Prefers slots in order (lowest slot number first).
   150→    Skips slots with uncommitted changes.
   151→
   152→    Args:
   153→        state: Current pool state
   154→        git: Git gateway for worktree operations
   155→        repo_root: Repository root path
   156→
   157→    Returns:
   158→        Tuple of (slot_name, worktree_path) for an available slot,
   159→        or None if no inactive slot found
   160→    """
   161→    assigned_slots = {a.slot_name for a in state.assignments}
   162→
   163→    # Get all worktrees from git (source of truth)
   164→    worktrees = git.worktree.list_worktrees(repo_root)
   165→
   166→    # Build lookup of slot_name -> worktree_path for managed slots
   167→    managed_worktrees: dict[str, Path] = {}
   168→    for wt in worktrees:
   169→        slot_name = wt.path.name
   170→        if extract_slot_number(slot_name) is not None:
   171→            managed_worktrees[slot_name] = wt.path
   172→
   173→    # Find first unassigned slot (by slot number order)
   174→    for slot_num in range(1, state.pool_size + 1):
   175→        slot_name = generate_slot_name(slot_num)
   176→        if slot_name in managed_worktrees and slot_name not in assigned_slots:
   177→            wt_path = managed_worktrees[slot_name]
   178→            # Skip slots with uncommitted changes
   179→            if git.status.has_uncommitted_changes(wt_path):
   180→                continue
   181→            return (slot_name, wt_path)
   182→
   183→    return None
   184→
   185→
   186→def is_slot_initialized(state: PoolState, slot_name: str) -> bool:
   187→    """Check if a slot has been initialized.
   188→
   189→    Args:
   190→        state: Current pool state
   191→        slot_name: Name of the slot to check
   192→
   193→    Returns:
   194→        True if slot is in the initialized slots list
   195→    """
   196→    return any(slot.name == slot_name for slot in state.slots)
   197→
   198→
   199→def find_branch_assignment(state: PoolState, branch_name: str) -> SlotAssignment | None:
   200→    """Find if a branch is already assigned to a slot.
   201→
   202→    Args:
   203→        state: Current pool state
   204→        branch_name: Branch to search for
   205→
   206→    Returns:
   207→        SlotAssignment if found, None otherwise
   208→    """
   209→    for assignment in state.assignments:
   210→        if assignment.branch_name == branch_name:
   211→            return assignment
   212→    return None
